scenario_name: Scenario 12 — Multi-File Review with Cross-File Bugs
justification: All pass criteria met: JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified (as High, not Critical, but with correct analysis of the auth bypass/crash risk), unauthenticated location endpoint identified as a privacy/safety issue, cross-file analysis performed (noted location endpoint lacks requireAuth used on other route), safe_to_deploy is false, and both fixes are specific and actionable.

observations.text: Identified unauthenticated location endpoint as a critical privacy and physical-safety risk with enumeration attack vector
Identified JWT_SECRET non-null assertion risk with correct analysis of dual failure modes (outage or auth bypass depending on library)
Provided specific fix for JWT_SECRET: fail-fast validation at startup
Provided specific fix for location endpoint: tracking token or requireAuth
Cross-file analysis went beyond the two expected findings to also identify IDOR on the authenticated route
safe_to_deploy is false with clear rationale
JWT_SECRET issue classified as High rather than Critical — rubric expects Critical since undefined secret may allow all tokens through

agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"IDOR vulnerability on authenticated route and unprotected location endpoint expose all delivery data to any caller"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Broken Object-Level Authorization (IDOR) on /deliveries/:id","risk":"Any authenticated user can read any delivery by guessing or enumerating IDs — no check that req.user owns the delivery","severity":"Critical","location":"delivery-routes.ts:4-6 (GET /deliveries/:id handler)","reproduction":"1. Authenticate

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified (severity High, not Critical, but the finding is substantive and correctly describes the risk), unauthenticated location endpoint identified as a privacy issue, cross-file analysis performed (noted location endpoint lacks auth used in other route), safe_to_deploy is false, and both fixes are specific and actionable.

observations.text: Unauthenticated location endpoint correctly identified as Critical with detailed reproduction steps and privacy/GDPR implications
JWT_SECRET non-null assertion risk identified with correct fix (fail-fast at startup)
Cross-file analysis performed — identified that location route lacks requireAuth middleware used elsewhere
Bonus finding: IDOR on the authenticated endpoint (no ownership check) — goes beyond the rubric requirements
Specific, actionable fixes provided for both findings including tracking token approach for location endpoint
JWT_SECRET issue rated High instead of Critical — rubric expects Critical since undefined secret may allow all tokens through

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified (labeled High rather than Critical, but the finding is thorough and accurate); unauthenticated location endpoint identified as a privacy/safety issue; cross-file analysis performed (noted location endpoint lacks requireAuth used elsewhere); safe_to_deploy is false; both fixes are specific and actionable.

observations.text: Identified all three key findings: IDOR, unauthenticated location endpoint, and JWT_SECRET non-null assertion risk
Excellent cross-file analysis — explicitly noted the location endpoint lacks the requireAuth middleware used in the other route
Fixes are specific and practical, including the fail-fast pattern for JWT_SECRET and ownership check patterns for the routes
Bonus IDOR finding on the authenticated route adds value beyond the rubric requirements
JWT_SECRET issue labeled as High rather than Critical — the rubric expects Critical since undefined secret may allow all tokens through
Production readiness section is thorough, noting the breaking change implications of adding auth to the location endpoint
